mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or rolling Rank IC stays below 0.02 over 3 years.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses the 4.5% threshold, labor market slack triggers recession fears that override growth expectations, causing sustained negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 60-day returns remain positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.5%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts signals robust consumer demand and construction activity, which drives corporate revenue growth and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative forward returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction term loses predictive power when tested against a baseline model.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production indicates strengthening supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces input cost pressures and boosts short-term equity returns during periods of stable monetary policy.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns when FEDFUNDS is rising, or the mechanism shows negative alpha during tightening phases.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend payouts, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon as earnings expectations adjust upward.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are negative in >60% of periods following positive GDPC1 revisions, or the correlation coefficient falls below 0.05 over rolling 5-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index breaches the 320.0 level, inflation expectations force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 returns remain positive in >70% of months where CPIAUCSL stays above 320.0, or the threshold effect disappears after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in >50% of observations, or the interaction term shows no incremental predictive value over single-series models.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly drop in housing starts signals a collapse in construction demand and consumer confidence, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as capital expenditure forecasts are downgraded.
failure_condition: Housing starts declines fail to predict negative 60-day returns in >65% of cases, or the signal loses significance when adjusted for interest rate changes.
